verses.text: The bride longs for her beloved's kisses, for his love is 'better than wine,' and asks to be drawn that they may run together. The soul thirsts for nearness to the beloved. So the Church longs for Christ, whose love outshines every earthly joy, and prays to be drawn to Him.

The bride longs for her beloved's kisses, for his love is 'better than wine,' and asks to be drawn that they may run together. The soul thirsts for nearness to the beloved. So the Church longs for Christ, whose love outshines every earthly joy, and prays to be drawn to Him.

The bride longs for her beloved's kisses, for his love is 'better than wine,' and asks to be drawn that they may run together. The soul thirsts for nearness to the beloved. So the Church longs for Christ, whose love outshines every earthly joy, and prays to be drawn to Him.

The bride longs for her beloved's kisses, for his love is 'better than wine,' and asks to be drawn that they may run together. The soul thirsts for nearness to the beloved. So the Church longs for Christ, whose love outshines every earthly joy, and prays to be drawn to Him.

Dark from the sun yet 'comely,' burdened by others to keep their vineyards while neglecting her own, she asks where her beloved feeds his flock. She owns her faults yet seeks him. The believer, conscious of failings, still longs to find the Shepherd of her soul.

Dark from the sun yet 'comely,' burdened by others to keep their vineyards while neglecting her own, she asks where her beloved feeds his flock. She owns her faults yet seeks him. The believer, conscious of failings, still longs to find the Shepherd of her soul.

The beloved praises her beauty, comparing her to a noble steed adorned with jewels. He delights in her and crowns her with ornaments. Christ sees His people lovely and adorns them with grace they did not earn.

The beloved praises her beauty, comparing her to a noble steed adorned with jewels. He delights in her and crowns her with ornaments. Christ sees His people lovely and adorns them with grace they did not earn.

At the king's table her perfume rises, and her beloved is to her 'a bundle of myrrh' she holds close all night. Love treasures the beloved's presence. To the faithful soul Christ is a fragrance carried always near the heart.

Each calls the other fair, their bed green and their house beams of cedar. Mutual delight crowns their love. So Christ and His people rejoice in one another, at home together in covenant love.
